report_name: Germany Faces Escalating Cyber Campaigns from Pro-Russian Hacktivists and Ransomware Groups

threat_actors.name: NoName057(16)
threat_actors.description: Pro-Russian hacktivist group conducting politically motivated denial-of-service and defacement campaigns against German organizations.

threat_actors.name: Dark Storm Team
threat_actors.description: Pro-Russian hacktivist collective waging politically motivated disruption campaigns against Germany, primarily through website defacement and denial-of-service (DoS) attacks.

threat_actors.name: Mr Hamza
threat_actors.description: Pro-Russian hacktivist collective waging politically motivated disruption campaigns against Germany, primarily through website defacement and denial-of-service (DoS) attacks.

threat_actors.name: Qilin
threat_actors.description: Financially motivated ransomware group targeting German organizations for extortion.

threat_actors.name: Akira
threat_actors.description: Financially motivated ransomware group targeting German organizations for extortion.

threat_actors.name: LockBit
threat_actors.description: Financially motivated ransomware group targeting German organizations for extortion.

attack_patterns.name: External Defacement
attack_patterns.description: Used by pro-Russian hacktivist groups to replace website content with their own messaging as a form of political protest against Germany.

attack_patterns.name: Network Denial of Service
attack_patterns.description: Used by hacktivist groups like NoName057(16) to flood German websites and services with traffic, making them inaccessible.

attack_patterns.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
attack_patterns.description: Used by hacktivist groups as an initial access method to exploit web server vulnerabilities for website defacement.

attack_patterns.name: Phishing
attack_patterns.description: Used by ransomware groups as a common initial access vector to steal credentials or deliver malware loaders to German organizations.

attack_patterns.name: Valid Accounts
attack_patterns.description: Used by ransomware groups to gain initial access by exploiting weak authentication on exposed internet-facing systems like RDP or VPNs.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: The final stage of ransomware attacks, where data is encrypted to extort money from German organizations.

attack_patterns.name: Inhibit System Recovery
attack_patterns.description: Ransomware groups use tools like vssadmin.exe to delete volume shadow copies, preventing easy recovery from the attack.

malware.name: Qilin Ransomware
malware.description: Ransomware used by the Qilin group in financially motivated attacks against German organizations.

malware.name: Akira Ransomware
malware.description: Ransomware used by the Akira group in financially motivated attacks against German organizations.

malware.name: LockBit Ransomware
malware.description: Ransomware used by the LockBit group in financially motivated attacks against German organizations.

tools.name: vssadmin
tools.description: Used by ransomware groups to delete volume shadow copies to inhibit system recovery.

identities.name: German Organizations
identities.description: Organizations in Germany targeted by both pro-Russian hacktivists and financially motivated ransomware groups due to the country's economic power and geopolitical stance.

infrastructure.name: Hacktivist Botnet
infrastructure.description: Botnets used by pro-Russian hacktivist groups to conduct Network Denial of Service (DDoS) attacks against German targets.

indicators.name: Potential DDoS Attack Traffic
indicators.pattern:
title: Potential DDoS Attack Traffic
id: 9c5b1a3d-e8f0-4b1a-9c8d-6f7a8b9c0d1e
status: experimental
description: Detects a large spike in inbound traffic (SYN, UDP, or HTTP floods) which could indicate a DDoS attack, as used by hacktivist groups.
references:
  - https://industrialcyber.co/news/germany-becomes-focal-point-of-escalating-dach-cyber-campaign-amid-ransomware-geopolitical-attacks/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-22
tags:
  - attack.t1498
level: high
logsource:
  category: firewall
detection:
  selection:
    action: 'allow'
  timeframe: 1m
  condition: selection | count() > 10000
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate high-traffic events
indicators.description: Detects a sudden, massive increase in traffic (especially SYN, UDP, or HTTP floods) to a public-facing service, a classic indicator of a DDoS attack.

indicators.name: Potential Web Defacement via FIM
indicators.pattern:
title: Potential Web Defacement via FIM
id: 8b4a092c-d7e1-4a09-8b7c-5e6d7c8b9a0b
status: experimental
description: Detects modification of core web page files, which is a strong indicator of a defacement attack.
references:
  - https://industrialcyber.co/news/germany-becomes-focal-point-of-escalating-dach-cyber-campaign-amid-ransomware-geopolitical-attacks/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-22
tags:
  - attack.t1491.002
level: high
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|endswith:
      - '\index.html'
      - '\index.htm'
      - '\index.php'
      - '\default.html'
      - '\main.js'
    Image|endswith:
      - '\w3wp.exe'
      - '\httpd.exe'
      - '\nginx.exe'
      - '\apache2.exe'
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate website updates by administrators.
indicators.description: An alert from a File Integrity Monitoring (FIM) system showing that a core web page file (e.g., index.html) has been modified is a strong indicator of a defacement attack.

indicators.name: VSSAdmin Shadow Copy Deletion
indicators.pattern:
title: VSSAdmin Shadow Copy Deletion
id: 7a39f81b-c6d2-49f8-8a6b-4d5c6b7a89f0
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of vssadmin.exe to delete volume shadow copies, a common ransomware TTP to inhibit system recovery.
references:
  - https://industrialcyber.co/news/germany-becomes-focal-point-of-escalating-dach-cyber-campaign-amid-ransomware-geopolitical-attacks/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-22
tags:
  - attack.t1490
level: critical
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\vssadmin.exe'
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'delete'
      - 'shadows'
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Rare legitimate administrative activity.
indicators.description: Detects the execution of vssadmin.exe to delete shadow copies, a technique universally used by ransomware groups to inhibit system recovery.
